condition_context: {"classification_trigger": "30-day past-due bucket", "purpose": "prudential classification of exposures"}
consequence: ADI must classify the exposure in the 30-day past-due category for prudential reporting purposes

condition_context: {"classification_trigger": "60-day past-due bucket", "purpose": "prudential classification of exposures"}
consequence: ADI must classify the exposure in the 60-day past-due category for prudential reporting purposes

condition_context: {"classification_trigger": "90-day past-due bucket", "purpose": "prudential classification of exposures"}
consequence: ADI must classify the exposure in the 90-day past-due category for prudential reporting purposes

condition_context: {"loan_type": "all", "additional_condition": "total amount unpaid outside contractual arrangements is equivalent to at least 90 days worth of contractual payments", "applies_to": "exposures subject to a regular repayment schedule"}
consequence: Exposure is classified as non-performing (default); ADI must treat the borrower as in default

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "non-performing (non-restructured)", "condition": "borrower has no exposure 90+ days past-due and borrower situation has improved"}
consequence: ADI may reclassify the exposure from non-performing to performing if all other criteria in paragraph 94 are also met

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "restructured non-performing", "condition": "repayments made as per revised contractual terms in a timely manner over the probation period"}
consequence: ADI may reclassify a restructured non-performing exposure to performing if all other criteria in paragraph 94 are also met

condition_context: {"exposure_type": "restructured", "condition": "all payments made in a timely manner as per revised contractual terms and borrower has resolved financial difficulty"}
consequence: ADI may exit the restructured exposure category once both exit criteria in paragraph 102 are met

condition_context: {"property_type": "standard residential or commercial property", "purpose": "fair value determination of property security"}
consequence: ADI must assume a marketing period of up to 12 months when determining the fair value of standard property security

condition_context: {"property_type": "specialised or unusual properties", "condition": "professional valuers advise that a longer period is appropriate"}
consequence: ADI may adopt a marketing period of up to 24 months for specialised or unusual properties when professional valuers advise this is appropriate

condition_context: {"category": "Category One", "description": "Well-secured exposures: first mortgage residential with 100% LMI, or LVR net of LMI <= 80% (valuation not older than 12 months), or qualifying second mortgage", "days_past_due": "any"}
consequence: No prescribed provisions are mandated for Category One exposures

condition_context: {"category": "Category Two", "description": "First mortgage residential, LVR net of LMI > 80% and <= 100%, valuation not older than 12 months", "days_past_due_min": 90, "days_past_due_max": 181, "term_of_payments_past_due": "90 days and less than 182 days"}
consequence: Minimum prescribed provision of 5% of balance outstanding applies

condition_context: {"category": "Category Two", "description": "First mortgage residential, LVR net of LMI > 80% and <= 100%, valuation not older than 12 months", "days_past_due_min": 182, "days_past_due_max": 272, "term_of_payments_past_due": "182 days and less than 273 days"}
consequence: Minimum prescribed provision of 10% of balance outstanding applies

condition_context: {"category": "Category Three", "description": "Personal and commercial loans (secured and unsecured), and residential mortgages with LVR net of LMI > 100%", "days_past_due_min": 273, "days_past_due_max": 364, "term_of_payments_past_due": "273 days and less than 365 days"}

consequence: Exposure qualifies as Category One (well-secured); no prescribed provisions mandated

condition_context: {"category": "Category Two", "loan_type": "residential_secured", "mortgage_type": "registered first mortgage", "condition": "loan is 90 days or more worth of payments past-due and valuation is not older than 12 months", "note": "greater than 80% but no more than 100%"}
consequence: Exposure classified as Category Two; minimum provision percentages from Table 1 apply based on term of payments past-due

condition_context: {"category": "Category Three", "loan_type": "residential_secured", "condition": "residential mortgage loans where LVR net of LMI exceeds 100%"}
consequence: Exposure classified as Category Three; minimum provision percentages from Table 2 apply based on term of payments past-due

condition_context: {"category": "Category Three", "loan_type": "residential_secured", "condition": "exposure secured by mortgage over residential property, 90+ days past-due, valuation not older than 12 months", "adjustment": "minimum provision percentage applied to outstanding balance less LMI minus 70% of security value"}
consequence: Provision may be adjusted to reflect part of the fair value of security; minimum provision percentage applied to the difference between outstanding balance less LMI and 70% of security value
